items.reason: The PR replaces the stale log_rate_limit section with the new request/access/diagnostic sample-rate semantics, derivation rules, and supported configuration paths.
The removed DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT client environment variable is deleted, matching the new worker-authoritative sampling model.
The dscli configuration table replaces log_rate_limit with request_sample_rate, access_sample_rate, diagnostic_sample_rate, and explains derivation semantics.
The new design doc describes the new LogSampler behavior, hot-path constraints, configuration model, RPC propagation, and verification cases.
This existing logging design doc still lists log_rate_limit and DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT and describes client LogRateLimiter sync, which the PR removes.
This trace doc still says SetRequestTraceUUID and SetTraceContext create decisions when local log_rate_limit is enabled and names LogRateLimiter as current behavior.
AccessRecorder now owns lazy sampled-out access-log facades and sampler checks before formatting, but this existing lifecycle doc was not updated.
This PLOG design still describes bypassing log_rate_limit/LogRateLimiter, but PLOG now participates in diagnostic_sample_rate semantics when not force-retained.
This implementation summary still says PLOG bypasses log_rate_limit and only the rate-limiter decision, which is stale after the LogSampler replacement.
